content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

Both amendments identify real, verified bugs. Amendment fr-3-78e7fc68 correctly identifies that bead_status blindly takes the first element from BrShowResponse::Many (line 956) while the same file already demonstrates the correct pattern using milestone_bead_refs_match filtering (lines 482-489), and the runtime struct has milestone_id available (line 234). Amendment fr-3-8da55614 correctly identifies a state synchronization gap where project select and run start/resume change the active project without updating active-milestone, leading to stale milestone resolution.

## Votes

- `fr-3-78e7fc68`: Accept
  Verified. Line 956 uses `details.remove(0)` to blindly pick the first element when `br show` returns multiple matches. The correct pattern — filtering with `milestone_bead_refs_match` — is already implemented in `request_bead_detail` at lines 482-489 of the same file. The `ProjectMilestoneControllerRuntime` struct (line 232-235) holds `milestone_id`, so `bead_status` has all the information needed to filter correctly but doesn't use it. If two milestones share a short bead ID prefix, the wrong bead's status is read, causing incorrect controller state transitions. The remediation is straightforward and follows an established pattern in the codebase.
- `fr-3-8da55614`: Accept
  Verified. `project select` (line 161-166) calls only `set_active_project` without touching `active-milestone`. `run start` (line 3371) and `run resume` (line 3543) likewise resolve only the active project. Meanwhile `resolve_active_milestone` (line 487-495) reads a persisted `active-milestone` file that may point to a milestone from a previously-active project. This creates a concrete state desync: switch to a project backed by milestone B, then `milestone next` still operates on stale milestone A. The proposed fallback — having `resolve_active_milestone` check the active project's `task_source.milestone_id` before returning `NoActiveMilestone` — is a clean, minimal fix that doesn't require threading milestone updates through every project-switching command.
